title: FLUID 0.7.2 — Semantic Truth Engine
description:
FLUID Data Product contract (v0.7.2). Semantic Truth Engine Release:

🔥 NEW in v0.7.2:
• Semantic model definitions - Optional semantics block on each expose for entities, measures, dimensions, and metrics
• Agent-consumable business logic - Eliminates semantic hallucination by providing machine-readable metric definitions
• Industry-aligned primitives - Compatible with dbt MetricFlow, Snowflake Semantic Views, and OSI-aligned formats
• Completes the agentic contract - semantics (how to act correctly) + agentPolicy (whether to act) = full agentic governance

✅ BACKWARD COMPATIBLE with v0.7.1:
• All v0.7.1 contracts remain valid (no breaking changes)
• semantics is optional on each expose - zero existing contracts affected
• All v0.7.1 features fully preserved (agentPolicy, sovereignty, orchestration)

🔥 Inherited from v0.7.1 (Provider-First Orchestration + Agentic Governance):

🔥 NEW in v0.7.1:
• Agentic governance - AI/LLM usage policies (agentPolicy) control which models can consume data and for what purposes
• Sovereignty constraints - Top-level jurisdiction and data residency requirements for compliance enforcement
• Orchestration philosophy - Pragmatic engine enumeration (v0.7.x) with path to capability-based abstraction (v0.8.0+)

🔥 NEW in v0.7.0:
• Provider actions as first-class tasks - Direct invocation of provider actions (aws.s3.ensure_bucket, sf.table.ensure) without wrapper operators
• Unified task type taxonomy - Simplified type system: provider_action, fluid_*, bash, python, sensor
• Data product references in datasets - Link trigger datasets to actual exposes[] with strong typing and auto-URI generation
• Task output → expose mapping - Formal produces[] declaration for automatic dataset registration and lineage
• Cross-provider workflow patterns - Native support for multi-cloud pipelines with XCom data passing
• Build step references - Link orchestration tasks to transformation steps via buildStepRef
• Data product dependencies - Tasks can declare dependencies on other data products with version constraints
• Enhanced error categorization - Retryable vs non-retryable errors with intelligent handling
• Provider-specific operators - Native support for SnowflakeOperator, BigQueryOperator, GlueOperator, etc.
• Cost tracking enhancements - Track actual vs estimated costs with budget enforcement

✅ BACKWARD COMPATIBLE with v0.5.7:
• All v0.5.7 contracts remain valid (no breaking changes)
• New features are opt-in via new fields
• Existing patterns (hybrid-reference, embedded-logic, multi-stage) fully preserved
• All data quality, observability, policy, and lineage features retained

📋 Inherited from v0.5.7:
• Multiple build patterns with conditional validation
• Enhanced build section for multi-modal concerns
• Comprehensive data quality specs with anomaly detection
• Detailed observability with default SLIs
• Fine-grained policy controls (privacy, RBAC, column-level access)
• Field-level lineage tracking
• Schema evolution strategies
• Machine learning model specifications
• Environment-specific overrides
• Simplified tagging maintained throughout

🎯 Design Philosophy:
• Provider actions = orchestration primitives
• Contracts drive both infrastructure and workflows
• Strong typing and validation
• Natural multi-cloud/multi-provider patterns
• Minimal abstraction layers
• Agentic governance: Data owners control AI/LLM usage boundaries
• Sovereignty-first: Jurisdiction constraints enforced at contract level

⚙️ Orchestration Philosophy (v0.7.x):
• Pragmatic approach: Enumerate known engines (airflow, dagster, prefect) for immediate adoption
• Trade-off: Implementation-specific vs pure abstraction - favoring real-world deployment patterns
• Future path: v0.8.0+ will evolve toward capability-based orchestration model
• Escape hatch: 'custom' engine allows experimentation without schema changes

examples.name: Customer 360 Analytics
examples.description: Unified customer profiles with ML-driven insights

fluidVersion.description: Contract schema version. Must be exactly '0.7.2' for semantic truth engine + agentic governance + provider-first orchestration with full backward compatibility to 0.7.1 and 0.5.7.

kind.description: Kind of contract. 'MLPipeline' added for basic ML support.

tags.description: Product-level tags for categorization and discovery.

labels.description: Product-level labels for metadata and automation.

oncall.description: Oncall rotation or contact for operational issues.

businessContext.description: Business alignment information for data mesh organization.

domain.description: Business domain this product belongs to.

subdomain.description: Business subdomain for more granular organization.

valueStream.description: Value stream this product participates in.

builds.description: NEW in v0.5.5: Multiple build configurations for multi-modal data products (e.g., batch + streaming, SQL + ML).

build.description: Single build configuration (legacy). Use 'builds' array for multi-modal.

lineage.description: Optional lineage tracking for better data governance.

schemaEvolution.description: Optional schema evolution strategy for managing changes.

machineLearning.description: Optional ML model specifications for MLPipeline kind.

sovereignty.description: NEW in v0.7.1: Jurisdiction and data residency requirements for compliance enforcement.

accessPolicy.description: Root-level access policy for automated IAM binding generation. Defines principals, permissions, and resources for data product access control.

identifier.description: Unified identifier pattern: starts/ends with alphanumeric or underscore, allows dots and hyphens in middle.

semverRange.description: Semver range (e.g., ^1.2, ~1.4, >=1.0.0). Not strictly validated by regex; tooling enforces.

tags.description: Simple string tags for categorization, discovery, and automation. Consistent pattern used throughout FLUID objects.

items.description: Tag format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, e.g., 'customer-data', 'pii', 'real-time'

labels.description: Key-value labels for structured metadata and automation. Consistent pattern used throughout FLUID objects.

additionalProperties.description: Label values are always strings for consistency and tooling compatibility.

exposeId.description: Stable interface handle (used by consumers & build.outputs).

description.description: Detailed description of the exposed resource.

tags.description: Expose-level tags for categorization and routing.

labels.description: Expose-level labels for metadata and automation.

semantics.description: NEW in v0.7.2: Semantic model definition mapping physical columns to business concepts. Defines entities, measures, dimensions, and metrics for agent-consumable business logic. Eliminates semantic hallucination by providing machine-readable KPI definitions. Compatible with dbt MetricFlow, Snowflake Semantic Views, and OSI-aligned formats.

schemaSignature.description: Hash of canonical schema/OpenAPI. Used by contract-tests gates.

dq.description: Enhanced data quality rules including anomaly detection.

tags.description: Contract-level tags for data quality and governance.

labels.description: Contract-level labels for automation and metadata.

sensitivity.description: Field-level sensitivity classification for improved policy ergonomics.

businessDefinition.description: Business definition and meaning of this field.

tags.description: Field-level tags for categorization and data governance.

labels.description: Field-level labels for metadata and automation.

tags.description: DQ rule tags for categorization and automation.

labels.description: DQ rule labels for metadata and routing.
QoS-level labels for automation and reporting.

agentPolicy.description: NEW in v0.7.1: AI/LLM usage governance - controls which AI models can consume this data and for what purposes.

fileFormat.description: Underlying file format for data files

sortOrder.description: Sort order specifications for data files

bindingLocation.description: Provider-native addressing with tightened structure for better interop.

defaultSLIs.description: Default SLI trio (freshness, completeness, latency) by kind.
Default SLI trio that CLI can auto-generate based on expose kind.

threshold.description: Freshness threshold (auto-set by kind: table=PT6H, stream=PT1M, api=PT1S)
Completeness threshold (auto-set by kind: table=0.95, stream=0.99)
P95 latency threshold (auto-set by kind: api=PT200MS, table=PT5S, stream=PT100MS)

replacement.description: Reference to replacement data product or expose.

agentPolicy.description: NEW in v0.7.1: AI/LLM usage governance. Controls which AI models can consume data and defines usage boundaries. Enables data owners to enforce 'reporting yes, training no' policies.

allowedModels.description: Whitelist of AI models permitted to consume this data. Examples: ['gpt-4', 'claude-3-opus', 'gemini-pro']. Empty array = no AI access.

deniedModels.description: Blacklist of AI models explicitly prohibited from consuming this data. Takes precedence over allowedModels.

maxTokensPerRequest.description: Maximum tokens per AI request. Prevents excessive data exposure in single queries.

maxTokensPerDay.description: Daily token limit for AI consumption. Enforces usage quotas.

allowedUseCases.description: Permitted AI use cases. Controls whether data can be used for training, inference, reasoning, etc.

deniedUseCases.description: Prohibited AI use cases. Example: Allow reporting but deny model training.

canReason.description: Whether AI agents can use this data for multi-step reasoning/chain-of-thought. False = simple retrieval only.

canStore.description: Whether AI systems can cache/store this data. False = ephemeral processing only.

retentionPolicy.description: Data retention requirements for AI systems consuming this data.

maxRetentionDays.description: Maximum days AI system can retain this data. 0 = no retention allowed.

requireDeletion.description: Whether AI system must delete data after use.

auditRequired.description: Whether AI consumption must be logged/audited.

purposeLimitation.description: Free-text description of allowed purposes. Example: 'Customer service chatbot only, not for marketing'.

sovereignty.description: NEW in v0.7.1: Data sovereignty and jurisdiction requirements. Declares WHERE data must reside and enables compliance enforcement before deployment.

jurisdiction.description: Required legal jurisdiction for data storage and processing. Used to validate binding.location matches sovereignty intent.

allowedRegions.description: Explicit list of allowed cloud regions. Example: ['eu-west-1', 'eu-central-1'] for EU-only.

deniedRegions.description: Explicit list of prohibited cloud regions. Takes precedence over allowedRegions.

dataResidency.description: Whether data must remain within jurisdiction boundaries at rest and in transit. True = strict residency.

crossBorderTransfer.description: Whether cross-border data transfer is permitted. False = data never leaves jurisdiction.

transferMechanisms.description: Allowed legal mechanisms for cross-border transfer (if permitted). Example: ['SCCs', 'BCRs', 'Adequacy'].

enforcementMode.description: How sovereignty violations are handled. strict = block deployment, advisory = warn, audit = log only.

validationRequired.description: Whether binding.location must be validated against sovereignty rules before deployment.

accessPolicy.description: Root-level access control policy for automated IAM binding generation. Defines who can access this data product and with what permissions.

grants.description: List of access grants to principals (users, groups, service accounts).

permissions.description: Permissions to grant. Will be mapped to provider-specific roles (e.g., BigQuery roles).

resources.description: Resources this grant applies to. JSONPath expressions referencing exposes[] items. If omitted, applies to all exposed resources.

conditions.description: Optional conditions for conditional access (e.g., IP restrictions, time windows).

consumeRef.$comment: productId identifies data product (renamed from 'provider' for clarity)

productId.description: Upstream data product identifier (e.g., 'silver.hr.people_v2'). Renamed from 'provider' for clarity.

maxStaleness.description: Maximum acceptable staleness for consumed data.

purpose.description: Business purpose for consuming this data.

build.description: Enhanced build configuration supporting v0.4.0 patterns with v0.5.5 improvements.

description.description: Human-readable description of this build configuration.

pattern.description: Build pattern: hybrid-reference (dbt-style), embedded-logic (raw SQL/code), or multi-stage (complex pipelines).

properties.description: Pattern-specific build properties with conditional validation.

outputs.description: List of exposeIds produced by this build.

dependencies.description: Dependencies on other builds (for multi-build orchestration).

transformations.description: Legacy transformations array for backward compatibility.

buildProperties.description: Base properties for all build patterns.

multiStagePattern.description: NEW in v0.5.5: Multi-stage orchestration pattern.

buildExecution.description: Build execution configuration from v0.4.0 enhanced.

datasets.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Can reference data products directly via exposeRef for strong typing and auto-URI generation. Backward compatible with raw URIs.

oneOf.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Data product reference with strong typing

productId.description: Data product ID to depend on

exposeId.description: Expose ID within the data product

model.description: Path/script identifier (dbt model, sql file, etc.)

outputs.description: List of exposeIds materialized by this step. VALIDATION: Tools must validate that every output exists in exposes[].exposeId.

upstream.description: Upstream dependencies and their field mappings.

impact.description: Impact level if this data product changes.

changeWindowDays.description: Advance notice period for breaking changes.

approvers.description: List of approvers for schema changes.

features.description: List of feature names used by this model.

tags.description: Airflow DAG tags (different from FLUID tags)

taskDefaults.description: Default configuration for all tasks (can be overridden per task)

errorHandling.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Enhanced error categorization and handling

retryableErrors.description: Error types that should trigger retries

nonRetryableErrors.description: Error types that should fail immediately

type.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Simplified task type taxonomy. Replaces 'operator' field (backward compatible). Types: provider_action (NEW!), fluid_validate, fluid_plan, fluid_apply, fluid_execute, fluid_verify, fluid_dq_check, bash, python, sensor, or provider-specific operators.

operator.description: DEPRECATED in v0.7.0: Use 'type' instead. Maintained for backward compatibility with v0.6.1 contracts. Maps to 'type' automatically (FluidExecuteOperator → fluid_execute, BashOperator → bash).

provider.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Provider name when type=provider_action. Examples: aws, gcp, azure, snowflake, databricks.

action.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Provider action when type=provider_action. Format: service.operation (e.g., s3.ensure_bucket, table.ensure, kinesis.create_stream).

buildStepRef.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Reference to transformation step in builds[].transformations[]. Links orchestration tasks to build steps for clearer mapping.

produces.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Declares what exposes[] this task produces. Enables automatic dataset registration and lineage tracking.

exposeId.description: The exposeId from exposes[] that this task produces

dataset.description: Optional: Airflow dataset URI. Auto-generated from binding.location if not specified.

updateStrategy.description: How this task updates the data

dataProductDependencies.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Tasks can declare dependencies on other data products with version constraints and freshness requirements.

productId.description: Data product ID to depend on

awaitFreshness.description: Maximum age of data to wait for (e.g., PT6H = 6 hours)

xcom.description: XCom configuration for inter-task communication (from v0.6.1)

trackActual.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Track actual costs vs estimate

alertThreshold.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Alert if actual > threshold * estimate (e.g., 1.5 = 150%)

allOf.description: Backward compatibility: If 'operator' is specified but not 'type', map operator to type

then.description: Operator field is deprecated but still works. Maps: FluidExecuteOperator→fluid_execute, BashOperator→bash, etc.

allOf.description: NEW in v0.7.0: Validate provider_action tasks have required fields

then.errorMessage: Tasks with type=provider_action must specify provider, action, and params

semanticModel.description: NEW in v0.7.2: Semantic model definition for an exposed interface. Maps physical columns to business concepts (entities, measures, dimensions, metrics) enabling AI agents and BI tools to generate correct queries without semantic hallucination. Compatible with dbt MetricFlow, Snowflake Semantic Views, and OSI-aligned formats.

name.description: Human-readable name for this semantic model.

description.description: Business context explaining what this semantic model represents.

defaultAggTimeDimension.description: Default time dimension used for measure aggregation. Can be overridden per-measure.

entities.description: Join keys with type annotations. Entities connect this table to others in the semantic graph.

type.description: Entity key type: primary (unique, complete), foreign (references another table), unique (unique, possibly partial), natural (real-world identifier).

expr.description: Column expression. Defaults to entity name if omitted.

measures.description: Aggregatable column expressions. Measures are the atomic building blocks from which metrics are composed.

agg.description: Aggregation function applied to the expression.

expr.description: SQL expression to aggregate. Defaults to measure name if omitted.

aggTimeDimension.description: Override the model-level default time dimension for this measure.

nonAdditiveDimension.description: Dimension over which this measure cannot be naively aggregated (e.g., account balances).

windowChoice.description: Which value to pick when the non-additive dimension is present.

createMetric.description: If true, automatically create a simple metric from this measure.

dimensions.description: Categorical and time-based grouping axes. Dimensions define how data can be sliced in queries.

type.description: Dimension type: categorical (text/enum grouping) or time (temporal axis).

expr.description: SQL expression. Defaults to dimension name if omitted.

timeGranularity.description: Default time granularity for this dimension.

metrics.description: Composable KPI definitions built from measures. Metrics are the named business calculations that agents and tools resolve.

type.description: Metric type: simple (single measure + filter), derived (expression over other metrics), ratio (numerator/denominator).

measure.description: Reference to a measure name (for simple metrics).

filter.description: SQL WHERE clause filter (e.g., "status = 'completed' AND is_refunded = FALSE").

inputMetrics.description: References to other metric names (for derived/ratio metrics).

expr.description: Mathematical expression combining input metrics (for derived metrics).

owner.description: Business owner of this metric definition.

tags.description: Semantic model tags for discovery and categorization.

labels.description: Semantic model labels for automation and metadata.

examples.name: orders_revenue_model
examples.description: Canonical semantic model for revenue analytics

metrics.name: net_revenue
metrics.description: GAAP-compliant net revenue: completed orders minus discounts, excluding refunds
